---
title: "Long-streaked Tussock Moth (Leucanopsis longa)"
slug: "leucanopsis-longa"
group: "Insecta"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623569956/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Long-streaked Tussock Moth"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Animal"
tax_class: "Insecta"
tax_order: "Lepidoptera"
tax_family: "Noctuoidea"
categories: ["Animal", "Insecta", "Lepidoptera", "Noctuoidea"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/623569956/medium.jpg" alt="Long-streaked Tussock Moth" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/sebento>
sebento</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/342424189">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Insecta
-   **Order:** Lepidoptera
-   **Family:** Noctuoidea

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=505582)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A fuzzy, light-colored moth characterized by elongated forewings with subtle streaking. In Florida, it is found in a variety of habitats including woodlands and suburban areas where its larvae feed on a range of plants. Adults are active during the warmer months and are attracted to light at night.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/505582-Leucanopsis-longa)
-   [Local profile](https://www.butterfliesandmoths.org/species/Leucanopsis-longa)
